local_evidence.summary: 37/37 tests passed after baseline anti-abuse checks were added.
Large-file report passed with baseline_ref 0e2a7ad8446a8f969b728553fa0138c8c1a0961a and no issues or warnings.

blocking_findings.summary: The initial CLI exposed --disable-baseline, which disabled all baseline anti-abuse checks.

non_blocking_findings: Additional test gaps existed for the disable-baseline path and some edge cases.
Plan status still showed LFA-P1.9 as review_pending during the review.

non_blocking_findings.summary: After CLI removal, the internal createLargeFileReport option still accepted disableBaseline; this was fixed after the PASS as defense in depth.

resolution.summary: The public CLI no longer accepts --disable-baseline, unknown flags fail closed, programmatic disableBaseline support was removed, and tests cover manifest ceiling increases, planned/accepted growth, new large-file registration bypass, total debt growth, near-threshold files, accepted-file rechallenge, and the removed CLI bypass.
